summaryLead: Mexico has lost more than 14,000 registered employers, and the count has fallen for two years. Tourism groups warn about fewer flights from the US. Volaris plans Starlink on more than 150 aircraft next year.
summaryNext: Next week brings US trade talks and new inflation and economic activity data on July 23.

stories.title: Registered employers have been falling for 24 months.
stories.context: El CEO reports a loss of more than 14,000 employers. Fewer formal employers can mean fewer formal jobs and a weaker tax base.

stories.title: Mexico is receiving fewer flights from the United States.
stories.context: Tourism groups say airline failures and operating restrictions are reducing international connections. That matters for tourism, business travel and airport traffic.

stories.title: Volaris plans to add Starlink internet to its fleet in 2027.
stories.context: The airline says more than 150 aircraft will receive satellite internet beginning next year.

companies.name: BYD
companies.title: Mexico's largest seller of hybrid and electric vehicles
companies.thesis: It sits at the center of Chinese investment in Mexico, EV adoption and the trade response from the United States.
companies.signal: BYD sells about four of every ten hybrid and electric vehicles in Mexico.

companies.name: Nu, Klar, Stori
companies.title: Digital lenders taking deposits from traditional banks
companies.thesis: The test is whether they lower the cost of credit and reach people banks do not while keeping bad loans under control.
companies.signal: With Finsus, they hold about 77% of Sofipo assets. The sector reported its first profit since 2017.

companies.name: Equifax / Círculo
companies.title: The data layer behind lending decisions
companies.thesis: Credit bureaus sit behind lending decisions made by banks, retailers and fintechs.
companies.signal: Equifax agreed to buy Círculo de Crédito at a US$750mn enterprise value, subject to approval.
